publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-08-21 18:00:40 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Correspondence relating to the development and administration of the library, general administrative files, administrative files of Librarians, financial records, statistical reports, and grant files.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence relating to the development and administration of the library, general administrative files, administrative files of Librarians Otto Kinkeldey and Stephen McCarthy, financial records, statistical reports, and grant files. Also, records pertaining to the construction of the John M. Olin Library, including correspondence and reports of the Ad Hoc Committee on the Library Building Program; preliminary drawings; architectural drawings and blueprints; booklets, brochures, and papers relating to the dedication ceremonies; Library of Congress files, 1958-1986; ALA and ARL files, 1949-1985; Collection Development and Management Project files and user survey, 1978-1981; file relating to Cornell's decision to join RLG, 1978-1979; scrapbook of clippings of events connected with the library, 1984-1990; results of a poll of library employees, ca. 1975; files of the Librarian (primarily Louis Martin and Gormly Miller) relating to departments in Olin Library including Circulation, History of Science, Icelandic, Interlibrary Loan, Manuscripts and University Archives, Maps, Microtexts, and Newspapers, the New York Historical Resources Center, and Reference, 1968-1989.
RLG files include materials relating to BibTech, Conspectus, Shared Cataloging Program, Preservation Committee, and Cultural Materials Alliance, ca. 1984-2006
Also recognition by the Council for Advancement and Support of Education (CASE) for superior accomplishments, 1983; and Libraries at Cornell brochure, 1984.

Also, photo album of a 1990 visit by Asian dignitaries, a 1980 User Survey, a code book of detailed work done by several departments (1891-1923) including special collection bookplates, and slides and audiocassettes describing the library.
Also Excellence in Academic Libraries Award certificate from ACRL (Association of College & Research Libraries) and library brochure.
Memorabilia includes pen used to sign an cooperation agreement with Tsinghua University in China, 2009 and various ribbons, buttons, etc.
Files of Karen Calhoun on the Library's Models for Academic Support (MAS 2010) project, 2002-2004; Library Management Team, 2001-2006; and Technical Services Executive Group files, 1999-2006.
Conservation and Preservation Department Grant files of Barbara Eden.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also Cornell University Library Records, #13-1-17. Seel also Cornell University Olin, Kroch, Uris Libraries administrative records, #13-1-3356.

emph.ref: Series I. Link to a partial finding aid

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Library Administration Records, #13-1-1082. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of office of origin with the exception of Boxes 58-70, 73.

controlaccess.subject: Academic librarians -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.

Library buildings.
Library architecture.
Library administration.

controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Library. Department of Rare Books
Cornell University. Department of Manuscripts and University Archives

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files are ordered chronologically by the dates of the official grant period. The files within each category therefore have dates both before and after the date of the grant, as preliminary and followup information is included in these files. In some cases where there are several files for one grant, the files are not arranged chronologically, but are instead grouped by complementary materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grant titles appear in bold, with corresponding folders below them.

unittitle.emph: Cornell/Xerox Joint Study in Digital Preservation
Cornell/Xerox Joint Study in Digital Preservation
Great Collection Microfilming Project, Phase III
Xerox: Testbed for Advancing Role of Digital Technology for Library Preservation and Access

did.unittitle: Testbed for Advancing Role of Digital Technology for Library Preservation and Access

unittitle.emph: Preserving Archival Material through Digital Tech: A Cooperative Demonstration Project

did.unittitle: Preserving Archival Material through Digital Tech: A Coop. Demo. Project Folder 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes grant approval documents and a catalog record for scanned brittle books.

did.unittitle: Preserving Archival Material through Digital Tech: A Coop. Demo. Project Folder 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Empty, with sticky note reading "Sent original letter to B. White (Townsend); Copies of rest to B. White and R. King"

did.unittitle: Preserving Archival Material through Digital Tech: A Coop. Demo. Project Folder 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposals, various handwritten notes, and correspondence between those involved in project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Department of Education L89-8303; Grant period January 1, 1993 - December 31, 1995

did.unittitle: Great Collection Microfilming Project IV Folder 1

unittitle.persname: Lynn, M. Stuart and Kenney, A.
unittitle.emph: Making of America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charles E. Culpeper Foundation L89-8511. This project focused on material from 1860 - 1960.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with the Culpeper foundation; a 1992 report about Phase I of the Cornell/Xerox Commission project; a summer 1992 copy of CIT News about electronic publishing; a Cornell booklet "Creating the Future" from 1991 outlining Cornell's goal for 75 million dollars in donations. Copies of this material were presumably sent to accompany the grant request for the Making of America project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Executive summary, a copy of the proposal, and financial reports.

Digital Training for Preservation Administrators - Folder 3

Digital Training for Preservation Administrators - Correspondence

unittitle.emph: Digital to Microfilm Conversions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NEH L89-8302; The order of the folders was determined by the grouping of material that supports each other rather than by date, as old projects are included to provide context for this new project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a bound version of the proposal.

did.unittitle: Central NY Preservation Technician Training Correspondence

unittitle.emph: Icelandic Collection (Fiske) - NEH

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains mostly preliminary information from the project's conception.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a 1992 survey report on the Icelandic collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a project narrative and additional budget information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Very preliminary budget estimates and drafts, including handwritten calculations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Informtion about The Icelandic Educational Network - ISMENNT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondences, monthly transaction statements, and accounting information. Also contains information concerning the "French Revolution Collection Recon (L89-8303).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondences, monthly transaction statements, and accounting information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: G. Under Vetlesen Foundation Contains a new proposal to the G. Under Vetlesen Foundation to supplement the project's NEH grant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains complete grant proposal and final report.

did.unittitle: Making of America - Mellon - Grant and Publications Folder 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings, correspondence, and additional grant proposal material.

did.unittitle: Making of America - Mellon - Grant and Publications Folder 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains proposal material and a March 1966 edition of the magazine American Libraries.

did.unittitle: Making of America - Mellon - Grant and Publications Folder 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains proposal material and the Kaleidoscope from January 1996.
